schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 22d0f8fabdcc656a7053e786d194acf188c01521
findings:
  - { id: judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's frozen wording asserts coverage is decided by 'the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies (a DECODABLE obligation.md at the convention path)'. The code diverges: VL-020 (vl020.go:74-77) is existence-only — it keys existingObligations on d.Kind=='obligation' at the RelPath and never inspects for_kind (its own doc: 'Existence only ... does not re-validate that file's own content'). The backstop (acceptobligation.go:127-147 via evidence.ObligationKindAt, which decodes for_kind) additionally requires present \u0026\u0026 forKind==kind, and REFUSES accept (exit 2) in two states VL-020 treats as covered/passing: (1) a present-but-undecodable file at the convention path, and (2) a decodable obligation at the exact convention path whose for_kind disagrees with its filename (VL-020 passes; it is VL-011, not VL-020, that reds this). So the predicate is provably STRICTER than VL-020, not 'the same', and it introduces refusal outcomes ac-2's scaffold-missing/skip-covered/byte-untouched framing does not contemplate. This is no longer a silent divergence: the spec body's round-3 and round-4 corrections now name it exactly (naming judged-coverage-predicate-forkind-keying, retracting the earlier 'can only refuse where VL-020 would pass' and 'VL-020 reds it' claims), and the code comments disclose it — the residual gap is that the FROZEN ac-2 text still overclaims full predicate-parity and is only 'queued for ratification precision'. Impact is narrow (the stricter arms can only refuse safely, and end-to-end accept still converges via VL-011/VL-020 at lint), so this is disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect, but the implementation's intent is deliberately stricter than the literal AC and the two are not equivalent as ac-2 states. (confidence 0.32)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind), which is safer: it catches at accept-time a misfiled/inconsistent obligation that VL-020's existence-only check would pass and that VL-011 independently reds at lint — one gate earlier, never freezing a story with an absent or internally-inconsistent obligation. The body disclosure now names the divergence exactly (round-3 + round-4 corrections, judge-acknowledged: 'no longer a silent divergence ... disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect'; confidence fell 0.50-\u003e0.42-\u003e0.32 as it converged). The sole residual is that the FROZEN ac-2 letter ('the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies') overclaims full parity when the backstop is stricter; frozen AC text cannot be edited in place (X-16), so its precision is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close), like init-wizard's ENOTEMPTY/os.Rename corrections. Terminal disposition per the loop policy — a disclosed-accepted honesty slice, not a fix-class finding." }
digest: sha256:60a020378500fe334ffba96924c637529f90344628052f0b90052b4900a8b6ba
integrity: sha256:dedaf8d681699936395083b016d844488e87ce9769fa4efda66f9faaa36f6d64
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've traced all five ACs against the implementation. ac-1 (preFlipHead-before-lint, identical stamp, addPaths staging), ac-3 (defer-rollback + unlink-on-!success), and the scaffold-before-lint ordering all hold as written. The one genuine semantic divergence is the ac-2 predicate-parity claim — confirmed against the code: VL-020 keys on existence-of-a-kind-classified-doc at the path (`vl020.go:74-77`, no `for_kind` inspection), while the backstop requires `forKind == kind` at the exact path plus two stricter refuse-arms (`acceptobligation.go:127-147`). This is now thoroughly and accurately disclosed in the spec body (rounds 3–4) and code comments, and the round-3 \"VL-020 reds it\" misreading has been corrected. The deviation from the *frozen literal AC text* nonetheless persists and is queued for ratification, so I surface it under its stable id for the disposition to track.\n\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate\",\"text\":\"ac-2's frozen wording asserts coverage is decided by 'the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies (a DECODABLE obligation.md at the convention path)'. The code diverges: VL-020 (vl020.go:74-77) is existence-only — it keys existingObligations on d.Kind=='obligation' at the RelPath and never inspects for_kind (its own doc: 'Existence only ... does not re-validate that file's own content'). The backstop (acceptobligation.go:127-147 via evidence.ObligationKindAt, which decodes for_kind) additionally requires present \u0026\u0026 forKind==kind, and REFUSES accept (exit 2) in two states VL-020 treats as covered/passing: (1) a present-but-undecodable file at the convention path, and (2) a decodable obligation at the exact convention path whose for_kind disagrees with its filename (VL-020 passes; it is VL-011, not VL-020, that reds this). So the predicate is provably STRICTER than VL-020, not 'the same', and it introduces refusal outcomes ac-2's scaffold-missing/skip-covered/byte-untouched framing does not contemplate. This is no longer a silent divergence: the spec body's round-3 and round-4 corrections now name it exactly (naming judged-coverage-predicate-forkind-keying, retracting the earlier 'can only refuse where VL-020 would pass' and 'VL-020 reds it' claims), and the code comments disclose it — the residual gap is that the FROZEN ac-2 text still overclaims full predicate-parity and is only 'queued for ratification precision'. Impact is narrow (the stricter arms can only refuse safely, and end-to-end accept still converges via VL-011/VL-020 at lint), so this is disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect, but the implementation's intent is deliberately stricter than the literal AC and the two are not equivalent as ac-2 states.\",\"confidence\":0.32}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/obligation-seam@22d0f8fabdcc656a7053e786d194acf188c01521, spec/obligation-seam@67a8643f30fc797295cd1c66245c9ec523d54ec9], digest: sha256:60a020378500fe334ffba96924c637529f90344628052f0b90052b4900a8b6ba, integrity: sha256:dedaf8d681699936395083b016d844488e87ce9769fa4efda66f9faaa36f6d64, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's frozen wording asserts coverage is decided by 'the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies (a DECODABLE obligation.md at the convention path)'. The code diverges: VL-020 (vl020.go:74-77) is existence-only — it keys existingObligations on d.Kind=='obligation' at the RelPath and never inspects for_kind (its own doc: 'Existence only ... does not re-validate that file's own content'). The backstop (acceptobligation.go:127-147 via evidence.ObligationKindAt, which decodes for_kind) additionally requires present && forKind==kind, and REFUSES accept (exit 2) in two states VL-020 treats as covered/passing: (1) a present-but-undecodable file at the convention path, and (2) a decodable obligation at the exact convention path whose for_kind disagrees with its filename (VL-020 passes; it is VL-011, not VL-020, that reds this). So the predicate is provably STRICTER than VL-020, not 'the same', and it introduces refusal outcomes ac-2's scaffold-missing/skip-covered/byte-untouched framing does not contemplate. This is no longer a silent divergence: the spec body's round-3 and round-4 corrections now name it exactly (naming judged-coverage-predicate-forkind-keying, retracting the earlier 'can only refuse where VL-020 would pass' and 'VL-020 reds it' claims), and the code comments disclose it — the residual gap is that the FROZEN ac-2 text still overclaims full predicate-parity and is only 'queued for ratification precision'. Impact is narrow (the stricter arms can only refuse safely, and end-to-end accept still converges via VL-011/VL-020 at lint), so this is disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect, but the implementation's intent is deliberately stricter than the literal AC and the two are not equivalent as ac-2 states. (confidence 0.32) — Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind), which is safer: it catches at accept-time a misfiled/inconsistent obligation that VL-020's existence-only check would pass and that VL-011 independently reds at lint — one gate earlier, never freezing a story with an absent or internally-inconsistent obligation. The body disclosure now names the divergence exactly (round-3 + round-4 corrections, judge-acknowledged: 'no longer a silent divergence ... disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect'; confidence fell 0.50->0.42->0.32 as it converged). The sole residual is that the FROZEN ac-2 letter ('the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies') overclaims full parity when the backstop is stricter; frozen AC text cannot be edited in place (X-16), so its precision is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close), like init-wizard's ENOTEMPTY/os.Rename corrections. Terminal disposition per the loop policy — a disclosed-accepted honesty slice, not a fix-class finding.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation
